OVERCOMING OBJECTIONS
By Russell Brunson

consultation with professional accounting, tax, legal, corporate finance, investment, banking, or other 
competent advisors.

The “Fair Enough” Close
___________, I would like you to take out a piece of paper and a pen to take some notes. Now
before we do that, I would like to cover something right up front. In front of you is a blank sheet
of paper, there is nothing on it, which means there is nothing to understand or misunderstand. I
am going to cover the ins and outs of our program along with the directions we take our
students in detail. However, __________, I would like to make you a promise.
I’m going to keep the explanation of the program just as simple and clear as that blank sheet of
paper in front of you. If I fail to do so, I will understand if you are reluctant to make a decision
concerning the program. And vice versa, if I can explain the nuts and bolts and you can see the
value and at that point you are unable to make a decision, I will be reluctant to extend an
invitation to you at all. For this reason, I promise you that when I finish explaining the program,
it will be just as clear and simple as the blank sheet of paper. At that point, I’m going to ask you
to make a decision. If you see that it is in your best interest to say yes, I am going to ask you to
say yes. If however, I don’t feel this program will help you accomplish the goals and dreams you
have set for yourself then we will, at that point, part as friends. Is that fair enough
___________? Great!
The Quality Close
Mr. /Mrs. _________A.D. Kessler/L2ST decided years ago that it would be easier to explain price
one time than to apologize for poor quality or poor service forever {pause}—and I’ll bet you are
glad he made that decision then, aren’t you?
The Price Is Too High Close
________, or company had to make a choice between building our product as cheaply as
possible and offering it as a get-by product, or building quality into the product for service,
durability, and your long lasting enjoyment. In short, for long-range value and benefit. With this
decision facing us, we tried to put ourselves in your shoes. We felt you would prefer to deal with
a company that puts everything possible into its product to make the best and most useful,
rather than a company that uses cheap materials and cheap labor to turn out a get-by product.
Most people, __________, and I’ll bet that includes you, clearly understand that good things are
not cheap and cheap things are seldom good. You want something you can depend on for good
service over the long haul don’t you, ___________?
www.getwsodownload.com

The Empathy Close
Tell me, ________, in addition to those things, would there be any other reason you could not
go ahead and make the commitment to secure your financial future, and the future of your
family, by getting started, working with a coach, in Creative Real Estate today?
Then whatever the objection they have given you, you need to feed exactly that objection back
to them.
*I don’t care what you sell, the odds are at least ten to one that the prospect’s reason for not
buying can be used as the reason he should buy.
www.getwsodownload.com

How much does this cost?
This program doesn’t cost anything if you qualify. The reason is we show you how to make it pay
for itself and keep on paying. For the people who don’t qualify, it costs them the rest of their
lives. They never get the opportunity to gain the knowledge to take control of their financial
future. Yes _________, there is tuition, but right now I don’t even know what your goals are, or
what you want to accomplish. Let me tell you, this is not a cookie cutter program. We tailor fit
the program to your needs and wants. So once we understand that, my Senior Director would
go over tuition and how the program will work. So if that was something you qualified for and
we could show you how to do it, would you be open to it?
PRICE/COST
*That is a good question. Write that down. I don’t know the answer, but if you qualify my
director (who is an expert) can answer that. 
*There would be tuition upfront. But we want to show you how to take care of it without taking
any money out of your pocket.
*I don’t know. But until I can ask you a few questions, I’m not sure if I can help you.
*If it’s not exactly right for you there is no charge. You’re not going to invest in something unless
it’s exactly right for you, correct?
*Is price your only concern? How much would you be willing to pay?
*If it was free would you invest? Why?
*Are you going to invest only if the price is right?
*We want to show you how to create a whole new career and how the business will more than
pay for itself.
Take Aways
*If all you’re concerned about is the cost, this may not be for you.
*You must see and understand the value before the director will even consider inviting you
aboard
www.getwsodownload.com
